name: Jalapeno Apricot Jelly
recipeIngredient: 1 large Red bell pepper (stems and seeds removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put jalapenos, bell pepper, and vinegar in a blender.
Puree until coarsely ground and small chunks remain.
2. Combine the apricots, sugar, and chili mixture in a large saucepan.
Bring to a boil.
3. Boil rapidly for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and skim off any foam that forms.
4. Allow the mixture to cool for 2 minutes. Then mix in pectin and food coloring (if using).
5. Pour into sterilized jars, seal, and cool.
Process in a boiling water bath for 10 minutes (3 pints) or 7 half-pint jars.
Note: Although the recipe calls for food coloring, the apricots, jalapenos, and red pepper combine to create an orange color on their own. I omitted the food coloring as it would not enhance the effect.
Gerlach writes: "This recipe calls for apricots, but peaches, nectarines, and pears work equally well. Any fresh green chile can also be substituted depending on your heat preference. Serranos will make it hotter; roasted, peeled New Mexican chiles will tame it down."
